reasoning: NOAA's Climate.gov is a standalone domain. While the Trump administration has been reported to be removing climate information from government websites (as it did during its first term), fully decommissioning a major .gov domain so that it no longer resolves or returns server responses is relatively rare. Usually, government IT practices dictate that retired sites are redirected (e.g., to an archive or a main agency page) to prevent link rot. 'Is It Down Right Now?' typically registers a site as 'UP' if the server responds to the domain request, even if it issues a 301 redirect or provides a stripped-down page. Furthermore, the domain has survived until May 7, 2025, which is several months into the administration. The window from the knowledge cutoff (May 21, 2025) to the resolution date (July 1, 2025) is only about six weeks. The likelihood of the domain being abruptly and completely taken offline—rather than repurposed, redirected, or left running with diminished content—within this specific short timeframe is low.
